Add the dry ingredients to the wet, and fold to mix until just combined. The dough WILL be sticky, do not be tempted to add more flour. It will firm up in the fridge, and the moisture is what is going to keep the donuts nice and soft in the middle, opposed to them drying out! Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and place into the refrigerator to chill for at least 1 hour. Mine was personally in the refrigerator for 1.5 hours!

While the dough is in the refrigerator, cut out 12-14 squares of parchment paper and spread them across 2-3 baking sheets. These will be used for the donuts to sit on once shaped and make it easier to transfer the donuts to the oil once ready to fry.

Then, prepare the glaze ahead of time so you are able to dip the donuts while they're still warm! In a large bowl, whisk together the powdered sugar, milk, melted butter, vanilla and salt until smooth. Set aside.

Once ready to cut the donuts, generously flour your work surface (very generously; this dough is sticky so please make sure to add enough flour to your work surface), along with flouring your hands and a rolling pin.

Dump the dough out onto your work surface, sprinkle the top with more flour, and gently roll/pat it out into a 1/2-inch thickness (do NOT knead or try to fold it, keep it in one place and just pat it out). Do not worry about the smoothness of the dough, the cracks, indentations, and crinkles are what create those signature cracks while frying! The dough should be very soft, so don't be afraid to use extra flour; MORE than you think.

Using a 3-3.5” donut cutter, cup, or mason jar lid, cut the donuts out. As you are cutting, dip the cutter into flour as needed to prevent it from sticking. Then, using a smaller 1” cutter or the wide end of a piping tip, cut out little holes in the middle of each donut. Save the little holes for donut holes or add back to the remaining dough to re-roll to cut out more donuts. You should get 12-14 donuts total depending on the size of the cutter you used.
Place the donuts and donut holes onto the prepared parchment paper squares on the baking sheets. Set aside while you heat the oil.
Fill a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven with oil, filling ~2” in the pan. Heat on medium-high until it reaches 360F-370F.
Line a wire cooling rack with a thick layer of paper towels.

Once the oil is hot enough, carefully transfer the parchment paper squares with the donuts on them slowly into the hot oil, 2-3 at a time. After 10-15 seconds, pull the parchment paper away from the donuts (it should almost immediately release). Fry the donuts for 1.5-2 minutes on each side (3-4 minutes total), until golden brown and cooked through. I HIGHLY recommend using a thermometer for the oil while frying, the temperature will need to be adjusted throughout the process as it does fluctuate. This will ensure that the oil is staying at the correct temperature, which prevents undercooked or overcooked donuts!
Using a slotted spoon, carefully remove the donuts from the oil and transfer to the rack with paper towels. Repeat with the remaining donuts.

While the other donuts are frying, dip the warm donuts into the glaze, making sure to generously coat both sides. Then place onto a cooling rack that's positioned on top of a baking sheet to let the glaze set.
Serve warm with a cup of coffee.
notes: The dough will be very sticky; do not add extra flour to the dough itself.
Use more flour than you think on your work surface when shaping.
A thermometer is highly recommended to maintain oil temperature between 360°F and 370°F.
The cracks and imperfections in the dough create the classic old-fashioned donut appearance.
Homemade cake flour: Measure 3 3/4 level cups of all-purpose flour. Remove 7 tablespoons of flour. Replace with 7 tablespoons cornstarch and sift together 4-5 times.
title: The Best Old-Fashioned Sour Cream Donuts

author: Kiley Heard
description: These Sourdough Krispy Kreme Donuts are incredibly soft, fluffy, and tender on the inside with a beautifully golden exterior that is just slightly chewy in the most irresistible way. Made with sourdough discard for a subtle tang and depth of flavor that takes these donuts completely over the top, then coated in a silky vanilla bean or strawberry glaze that gets perfectly crackly on the outside. Served warm with a hot cup of coffee - these donuts are the ultimate homemade treat that rivals your favorite Krispy Kreme every single time!
equipment: 3-inch donut cutter, cup, or mason jar lid
Large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven
ingredients: 3/4 cup (180ml) milk of choice, warmed to ~110F
1/2 cup (140g) sourdough discard, at room temperature
1/2 cup (113g) salted butter, melted and slightly cooled
1 tsp (4g) vanilla bean paste or extract
1 cup (226g) salted butter, melted and slightly cooled
1 1/2 tsp (6g) vanilla bean paste or extract
instructions: Start by activating the yeast for the dough. Pour the warm milk into a small bowl or glass measuring cup. Add 1 tbsp (12g) of the granulated sugar and sprinkle the yeast overtop. Stir and allow to sit for 5-7 minutes, until frothy and activated. If using instant yeast, no need to wait for it to bloom; go straight into mixing the dough together!
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour and salt. Set aside.

Once activated, pour the yeast mixture into the bowl of a stand mixer (or a large bowl if mixing/kneading the dough by hand). Add the rest of the sugar (1/4 cup minus the 1 tbsp that was used to activate the yeast), sourdough discard, melted and cooled butter, eggs, vanilla, and the flour mixture.
With the dough hook attachment (or with a wooden spoon if mixing by hand), mix on low speed for 1 minute, until barely combined. Allow to rest for 5 minutes so the flour can soak up the liquids.

Then, mix on medium speed for 5-7 minutes, until the dough is elastic, smooth, and is pulling away from the sides of the bowl. If the dough seems a little too sticky, you can add 1 tbsp more flour at a time, until it starts to pull away from the sides of the bowl. Don't add too much, you want the dough to be slightly sticky! I ended up adding ~2 tbsp more flour. If you are mixing and kneading by hand, pour the dough onto a floured surface and knead for 5-7 minutes by hand.
Transfer the dough to a lightly oiled bowl. Cover and allow to rise in a warm environment for 1-1.5 hours, or until doubled in size. I did a full 1.5 hours!

Once the dough is almost finished rising, cut out 10-12 squares of parchment paper and spread them across 2 baking sheets. These will be used for the donuts to sit on once shaped and make it easier to transfer the donuts to the oil once ready to fry.

Once the dough is doubled, punch it down and turn it onto a lightly floured surface. Roll the dough out to ~1/2” thickness. Using a 3” donut cutter, cup, or mason jar lid, cut the donuts out. Then, using a smaller 1” cutter or the wide end of a piping tip, cut out little holes in the middle of each donut. Save the little holes for donut holes or add back to the remaining dough to re-roll to cut out more donuts. You should get 10-12 donuts total.
Place the donuts onto the prepared parchment paper squares on the baking sheets. Loosely cover with a kitchen towel and allow to proof for 30-45 minutes, until puffy.

While proofing, make the glaze(s). Start with the vanilla bean glaze. In a large bowl, whisk together the powdered sugar, milk, melted butter, vanilla, and salt, until well combined and smooth. If also making a strawberry glaze, pour half of the plain glaze into a separate bowl. Add the crushed freeze-dried strawberries and whisk to combine.
When the donuts are almost done proofing, fill a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven with oil, filling ~2” in the pan. Heat on medium-high until it reaches 350F-365F.
Line a wire cooling rack with a thick layer of paper towels.

Once the oil is hot enough, carefully transfer the parchment paper squares with the donuts on them slowly into the hot oil, 2-3 at a time. After 10-15 seconds, pull the parchment paper away from the donuts (it should almost immediately release). Fry the donuts for 1-2 minutes on each side (2-4 minutes total), until golden brown.
Using a slotted spoon, carefully remove the donuts from the oil and to the rack with paper towels. Repeat with the remaining donuts.

While the other donuts are frying, dip the warm donuts into the glaze(s), making sure to generously coat both sides. Then place onto a cooling rack that's positioned on top of a baking sheet to let the glaze set.
Serve warm with a cup of coffee.
notes: Active sourdough starter may be used; see FAQs on the original recipe.
If using cups, scoop and level flour properly to avoid overmeasuring.
If your yeast won't activate, verify that it is not expired.
Milk should be about 110°F when activating the yeast.
Use a thermometer to maintain oil between 350°F and 365°F.
title: Sourdough Krispy Kreme Donuts (with Discard)

author: Kiley Heard
description: These Homemade Honey Buns are soft, pillowy, and tender in the center with a perfectly golden, slightly chewy exterior. Filled with warm, gooey swirls of honey butter and cinnamon brown sugar, then fried to golden perfection and coated in a silky honey glaze, these bakery-style honey buns are best enjoyed warm with a hot cup of coffee.
equipment: Large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven
ingredients: 3/4 cup (180ml) milk of choice, warmed to 110F
1/2 cup (120g) Greek yogurt or sour cream, at room temperature
1/2 cup (113g) salted butter, very softened (almost melted)
1 tsp (4g) vanilla bean paste or extract
Avocado oil (or neutral oil of choice), for frying
1 tsp (4g) vanilla bean paste or extract
1/2 cup (110g) brown sugar, light or dark
1 tsp vanilla bean paste or extract
instructions: Start by activating the yeast for the dough. If using instant yeast, do not follow these instructions for activating the yeast, see the notes section below. Pour the warm milk into a small bowl or glass measuring cup. Add 1 tbsp (12g) of the sugar and sprinkle the yeast overtop. Stir and allow to sit for 5-7 minutes, until frothy and activated.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour and salt. Set aside.

Once activated, pour the yeast mixture into the bowl of a stand mixer (or a large bowl if mixing/kneading the dough by hand). Add the rest of the sugar (1/2 cup minus the 1 tbsp that was used to activate the yeast), Greek yogurt or sour cream, butter, eggs, vanilla, and the flour mixture.
With the dough hook attachment (or with a wooden spoon if mixing by hand), mix on low speed for 1 minute, until barely combined. Allow to rest for 5 minutes so the flour can soak up the liquids.

Then, mix on medium speed for 5-7 minutes, until the dough is elastic, smooth, and is pulling away from the sides of the bowl. If the dough seems a little too sticky, you can add 1 tbsp more flour at a time, until it starts to pull away from the sides of the bowl. Don't be tempted to add too much, you want the dough to be slightly sticky! I ended up adding about 1-2 tbsp more flour. If you are mixing and kneading by hand, pour the dough onto a floured surface and knead for 7-9 minutes by hand, or until smooth and elastic.
Transfer the dough to a lightly greased bowl. Cover and allow to rise in a warm environment for 1-1.5 hours, or until doubled in size. I did a full 1.5 hours!
While the dough is rising, prepare the fillings. For the honey butter filling, mix together the softened butter, honey, vanilla, and salt in a medium bowl, until well combined. Set aside.
For the cinnamon brown sugar filling, mix together the brown sugar and cinnamon in a small bowl. Set aside.

Once the dough is almost finished rising, cut out 12 squares of parchment paper and spread them across 2-3 baking sheets. Lightly dust each square of parchment paper with flour. These will be used for the honey buns to sit on once shaped and make it easier to transfer the buns to the oil once ready to fry.
Once the dough is done rising and is doubled, punch it down and turn it onto a floured surface. Roll the dough out to a 15x24” rectangle.

Disperse the honey butter filling all over the surface of the dough, and spread/smooth it out evenly, leaving a very small border around the edges. Then, sprinkle the cinnamon brown sugar filling all over the surface, on top of the honey butter. Use your hands to slightly press the cinnamon brown sugar filling into the honey butter.
On the long end, use a sharp knife or pizza butter to cut the dough into 12 equal strips, each strip should be 2” in width and 15” long.

Then starting on the short end for each strip, tightly roll them up into a coiled roll, and tuck the last 1” of the strip under the bottom side of the roll, pinching to seal. PLEASE make sure to roll tightly and pinch very well and tightly, this will ensure that the honey buns will not unravel when frying.
Place the buns onto the prepared parchment paper squares on the baking sheets. Then, use a piece of parchment paper to place on top of each bun and press down to slightly flatten.
Loosely cover the buns with a kitchen towel and allow to proof for 30-45 minutes in a warm environment, until puffy but not doubled in size.

While the buns are proofing, prepare the honey glaze. In a large bowl, whisk together the powdered sugar, melted butter, honey, milk, vanilla, and salt until smooth. If the mixture seems a little thick, add 1 tbsp (15ml) of milk at a time.
Once the buns are almost done proofing, fill a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven with oil, filling ~2” in the pan. Heat on medium-high until it reaches 360F-370F.
Line a wire cooling rack with a thick layer of paper towels.
If you really want to ensure that the buns don't unravel, you can take a toothpick and push it into the seam end of each donut to secure the seam in place while frying.

Once the oil is hot enough, carefully transfer the parchment paper squares with the buns on them slowly into the hot oil, 2-3 at a time. I recommend inverting them into the oil, top-side down, so the parchment paper is on the top (this will help to keep them together). After 10-15 seconds, pull the parchment paper away from the buns (it should almost immediately release). Fry the donuts for 1-2 minutes on each side (2-4 minutes total), until golden brown. *NOTE: some of the filling will leak out as they cook - this is completely normal!*
Using a slotted spoon, carefully remove the buns from the oil and to the rack with paper towels. Repeat with the remaining buns.

While the other buns are frying, dip the warm buns into the glaze, making sure to generously coat both sides. Then place onto a cooling rack that's positioned on top of a baking sheet to let the glaze set.
Serve warm with a cup of coffee.
notes: If using instant yeast, do not activate it separately. Add it directly to the dry ingredients and use slightly warmed milk.
I HIGHLY recommend weighing your ingredients always (it's much more accurate).
If using cups, scoop and level the flour properly to avoid over-measuring.
A thermometer helps maintain proper frying temperature.
Some filling leaking during frying is completely normal.
title: Homemade Honey Buns

author: Kiley Heard
description: These Nutella-Stuffed Banana Bread Chocolate Chip Cookies (with Brown Butter) are soft and gooey in the center with perfectly golden, crisp, crinkly edges. Each cookie is stuffed with melty Nutella and studded with chocolate chips, while ripe banana pairs beautifully with the nutty, toasted flavor of brown butter. Best served warm with flaky sea salt and a cold glass of milk.
ingredients: 1 tsp (4g) vanilla bean paste or extract
1 cup (200g) chocolate chips, chunks, or chopped chocolate of choice (I used a mixture of dark, semi-sweet, and bittersweet)
9-10 tbsp Nutella (or chocolate hazelnut spread of choice)
instructions: Start by freezing the Nutella for the filling. Line a small baking sheet or plate with parchment paper. Scoop out full tablespoons of Nutella and place them onto the parchment paper (I would do 10 and it's okay if they slightly flatten out to discs). Place into the freezer for at least 30 minutes, or until solid.

Brown the butter. Add the butter to a saucepan over medium-high heat. Allow to melt and brown, while stirring continuously (~5-8 minutes). As the butter continues to cook, you'll notice that it starts to foam up across the surface (after about 2 minutes). Stir frequently to prevent the milk solids from burning. Continue cooking, stirring with a spatula, until the milk solids begin to brown (the brown specks at the bottom of the pan) and the color starts to deepen. Once the brown butter reaches a deep golden amber color and smells nutty, remove from the heat and immediately pour into a large bowl. Set aside to cool slightly (about 15-20 minutes), enough so it doesn't melt the sugars when added, which will ensure that the cookies don't spread too much.
Line a baking sheet with parchment paper to use later.
In a separate medium bowl whisk together the flour, baking soda, salt, and cinnamon. Set aside.
Once the brown butter has cooled, add both sugars and whisk until well combined.
Then add in the egg, mashed banana, and vanilla. Whisk until well combined. The mixture should almost appear and smell like caramel at this point.
Add the dry ingredients to the wet, and gently fold with a spatula until just combined.

Add the chocolate chips/chunks, and gently fold until evenly dispersed. Make sure to check the bottom of your bowl for any remaining flour, making sure that it is all fully combined. The cookie dough will be pretty soft, this is normal!
At this point, remove the frozen Nutella portions from the freezer.

Use a large cookie scoop (#20; 3 tbsp; 2oz) to scoop out portions of the cookie dough. Use your fingers to slightly flatten out each scoop of cookie dough, then place a frozen Nutella ball into the center of each. Use your fingers to enclose the Nutella filling with the cookie dough (you can add an extra small cookie scoop of cookie dough if needed to ensure that the Nutella filling is completely enclosed). Use your hands to shape into a ball and place onto the prepared baking sheet. Repeat with the remaining cookie dough and Nutella filling portions.
Once all of the cookie dough balls are assembled on the baking sheet, cover with plastic wrap and place into the refrigerator to chill for at least 1 hour.
Once ready to bake, preheat the oven to 350F. Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper.
Space the cookies on the baking sheet 2” apart from each other. I did 5 cookies at a time.

Bake at 350F for 12-15 minutes, until the center is slightly puffed up, lightly golden (they should look almost underbaked), and the edges are golden and set. Remove from the oven and if needed, immediately use a bowl or large round cookie cutter to shape into the ‘perfect' circle. You can press extra chocolate chips or chopped chocolate into them immediately after removing them from the oven to get the melty chocolate on top (I did this).

The cookies will continue to bake on the baking sheet while they are cooling, so don't be afraid to pull them right at the 12 minute mark. If you like your cookies more done, keep them in the oven for 1-2 more minutes.
Allow the cookies to cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes before transferring to a wire cooling rack.
Serve warm with a sprinkle of flaky sea salt and a cold glass of milk, if desired.
notes: The BIGGEST tip I can give to make these cookies perfect every time is to weigh or scoop and level your flour properly.
I highly recommend weighing ingredients because it is much more accurate.
If measuring with cups, make sure to scoop and level the flour correctly to avoid overmeasuring.
title: Nutella-Stuffed Banana Bread Chocolate Chip Cookies (with Brown Butter)

author: Kiley Heard
description: These Heart Churros With Homemade Chocolate Sauce are golden and crisp on the outside, tender in the middle, and coated in strawberry cinnamon sugar for a festive pink Valentine's Day twist. Shaped into hearts and served warm with a rich homemade chocolate dipping sauce, these fun, romantic churros are the perfect sweet treat for celebrating love.
equipment: Electric hand mixer or stand mixer with paddle attachment
Large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven
ingredients: 1/2 cup (113g) salted butter, diced into cubes
1 tsp (4g) vanilla bean paste or extract
Avocado oil (or neutral oil of choice), for frying
Optional: 1 oz freeze-dried strawberries, crushed or blended into a powder
1/2 cup (100g) milk chocolate chips or chopped chocolate
1/2 cup (100g) dark chocolate chips or chopped chocolate
1/4 cup (60ml) milk of choice (whole milk recommended)
instructions: Start by preparing the dough. In a medium saucepan over medium-high heat, add the water, butter, sugar, vanilla, and salt. Mix and bring to a small rolling boil, while you stir occasionally.

As soon as the butter is melted and it starts to boil, turn the heat to low and add the flour. IMMEDIATELY and vigorously stir with a spatula or wooden spoon for 1-2 minutes, until the flour is fully incorporated. The dough will become a thick, smooth paste, leaving a slight film on the bottom of the pan.

Remove from the heat and scrape the dough into a large bowl or the bowl of a stand mixer. Allow to cool for at least 10-15 minutes (it needs to be cooled enough so when you add the eggs, the dough won't cook them).
While cooling, prepare the cinnamon sugar mixture by mixing together the sugar, cinnamon, and crushed freeze-dried strawberries (if using) in a small bowl. Set aside.

Using an electric hand mixer (or mix by hand with a spatula) or a stand mixer with the paddle attachment, beat the eggs into the dough until completely incorporated. The dough should become smooth, glossy, and sticky.
Transfer the dough to a piping bag fitted with
Fill a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven with oil, filling ~2” in the pan. Heat on medium-high until it reaches 360F-375F.
Line a wire cooling rack with a thick layer of paper towels.
While the oil is heating, cut out small squares of parchment paper (about 3x3”). These will be used for the churros to be piped on and make it easier to transfer them to the oil once ready to fry.

Pipe 3” churros onto each piece of parchment paper, making any shape desired. I did a heart shape, but you can certainly do any shape you want! You can alternatively pipe the dough over the oil and use clean kitchen scissors to snip off the dough every 3 inches; I prefer the parchment paper method!

Once the oil is hot enough, place 3-4 pieces of parchment paper with the churros on them slowly into the oil. After 10-15 seconds, pull the paper away from the churros with metal tongs (it should almost immediately release).
Fry the churros for about 2-4 minutes (~1-2 minutes on each side), until crispy golden brown.
Using a slotted spoon, carefully remove the churros from the hot oil and set on the rack with paper towels. Then, quickly toss and coat the churros in the cinnamon sugar mixture.

For the chocolate sauce, add both chocolates, heavy cream, milk, and salt to a small saucepan over medium heat. Continuously stir and heat until the chocolate is completely melted and the mixture is smooth. Transfer to a serving bowl or container.
Serve the churros warm with the homemade chocolate sauce.
notes: Any shape can be used, though hearts are shown in the recipe.
The parchment paper method makes transferring the churros to the oil easier.
Whole milk is recommended for the chocolate sauce.
You can pipe directly over the oil and cut with kitchen scissors instead of using parchment squares.
Strawberry powder is optional and adds a pink Valentine's Day twist.
title: Heart Churros with Homemade Chocolate Sauce

author: Kiley Heard
description: These Cinnamon Crunch Blueberry Butter Swim Biscuits are soft, fluffy, and unbelievably buttery, with juicy, tart blueberries bursting through every bite. Baked until golden and topped with a brown sugar cinnamon crunch for the perfect contrast of texture, these cozy biscuits are best served warm with a pat of butter, flaky sea salt, and a hot cup of coffee.
ingredients: 1 1/2 cups (360ml) buttermilk, cold [substitution: 1 1/2 cups (360ml) milk of choice mixed with 1 1/2 tbsp (22ml) apple cider vinegar or lemon juice]
1 tsp (4g) vanilla bean paste or extract
instructions: Preheat the oven to 450F.
Start by preparing the biscuit dough. In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Then add the blueberries and toss until evenly dispersed. Set aside.
In a separate medium bowl, whisk together the buttermilk, Greek yogurt, and vanilla until well combined.

Make a well in the center of the flour mixture, and pour in the wet mixture. Use a wooden spoon or spatula to stir until just combined, being careful not to overwork the dough. The dough WILL be sticky and almost wet, but trust the process!

Add the butter to a 10.5x7.5”, 8x8” or 9x9” baking pan or dish. If using a square baking pan, I recommend a 9x9”. Place the pan into the oven (it's okay if it's still preheating), and allow the butter to sit in the oven for a few minutes to fully melt. I like to let it sit until it's just starting to brown for a little extra toastiness!
While the butter is melting in the oven, prepare the cinnamon crunch topping by mixing the brown sugar and cinnamon together in a small bowl. Set aside.
Remove the pan from the oven and pour/drop the biscuit dough into the hot butter. Use a spatula to spread the dough evenly out to the edges of the pan.

Using a knife or a bench scraper, score the unbaked dough into 6 or 8 equal rectangles or squares, depending on how large you want the biscuits. As you do this, try to almost drag the butter down with the score, this will help to separate the biscuits as they bake.
Then, sprinkle the cinnamon brown sugar all over the tops of the biscuits.

Place the pan onto the middle rack of your oven, and place a baking sheet on the rack below to catch any drippings. Bake at 450F for 25-30 minutes, or until the biscuits are deeply golden, crispy, and bubbling.
Remove the biscuits from the oven and let rest for at least 5-10 minutes so the butter can continue to absorb.
Serve the biscuits warm with some butter, flaky sea salt, and a hot cup of coffee, if desired.
notes: Frozen blueberries can be used in place of fresh.
Milk mixed with apple cider vinegar or lemon juice can substitute for buttermilk.
The dough will be sticky and almost wet; this is expected.
Allowing the butter to brown slightly adds extra flavor.
Let the biscuits rest 5-10 minutes after baking so the butter can absorb.
title: Cinnamon Crunch Blueberry Butter Swim Biscuits

author: Kiley Heard
description: This Vanilla Bean Cheesecake with White Chocolate Mousse starts with a buttery graham cracker crust for the perfect crunch, followed by a creamy, tangy vanilla bean cheesecake filling that lets the vanilla truly shine. It's finished with a lush layer of white chocolate mousse and served with a vibrant berry compote for a beautifully balanced, elegant dessert.
ingredients: 3/4 cup (180g) Greek yogurt or sour cream, at room temperature
1/3 cup (80ml) heavy whipping cream, at room temperature
1 cup (130g) mixed berries, fresh or frozen
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350F. Grease a 9” springform pan or cheesecake pan with butter, then line the bottom with a parchment paper circle. Tightly wrap the bottom of the springform pan with at least 2-3 layers of foil. I also like to use a few slow cooker liners and place them around the bottom to really prevent water from seeping into the bottom of the pan later on. Set aside.
In a medium bowl, stir together the graham cracker crumbs and melted butter until the crumbs are coated in the butter and resemble wet sand.

Press the crumbs into the bottom and 3/4 of the way up the sides of the prepared pan, using a small measuring cup to flatten and smooth out. Bake at 350F for 10-12 minutes. Then remove from the oven and let cool while you make the cheesecake filling. Reduce the oven temperature to 325F.
Set a large pot of water on the stove over high heat to start boiling. This will be used later when baking the cheesecake.
In a large bowl, beat the cream cheese with a whisk or handheld electric mixer to smooth. Add the sugar and beat for another minute until well combined, creamy and smooth.

Whisk in the eggs, one at time, adding the next egg only after the previous is fully incorporated, scraping down the sides of the bowl as needed. When adding the eggs, I like to switch to whisking by hand to prevent over-mixing the batter.
Once all of the eggs are incorporated, add the Greek yogurt (or sour cream), heavy cream, vanilla, and salt. Whisk until well combined. At this point, the batter should be very smooth and creamy!

Pour the cheesecake batter on top of the crust in the pan. Bang the pan on the counter to release some air bubbles. Then pop the bubbles that rise to the surface with a small sharp knife. I repeat this 3 to 5 times!
Place the cheesecake pan in the center of a larger roasting pan. Then pour the boiling/hot water into the larger roasting pan until it reaches about halfway up the sides of the cheesecake pan.
Carefully transfer the cheesecake to the oven. Bake at 325F for 80-90 minutes, or until the edges have set but the middle still slightly jiggles when shaken.

Once done, turn the oven off and prop a wooden spoon in the oven door to keep it cracked. Leave the cheesecake in the warm oven for 1 hour. Then, remove from the oven and the roasting pan, and allow it to cool to room temperature (~1-2 hours). After cooled, cover the top of the cheesecake pan tightly, and place into the refrigerator to chill for at least 6 hours or overnight.

About 1 hour before you're ready to serve, prepare the white chocolate mousse. Place the chopped white chocolate in a small heatproof bowl and microwave for 30 seconds. Give it a stir and then continue to microwave at 15-second intervals, stirring after each interval, until the white chocolate is fully melted. Set aside.
In a separate large bowl, combine the cream cheese, melted white chocolate, sugar, and vanilla. Use a handheld electric mixer or whisk to beat until creamy and fully combined. Set aside.

Add the heavy whipping cream to the bowl of a stand mixer a stand mixer (or mixing bowl). Use the whisk attachment or a handheld electric mixer, and beat the heavy whipping cream, starting on low speed and increasing to high slowly as it begins to firm up. Beat for 60-90 seconds until stiff peaks form, stopping once halfway through to scrape down the sides of the bowl.
Add the whipped cream to the bowl with the white chocolate/cream cheese mixture and gently fold it in until fully incorporated.
Spread the white chocolate mousse all over the top of the cheesecake into an even layer. Place the cheesecake back into the refrigerator for at least 1 hour to allow the mousse to fully set.

While the cheesecake is in the refrigerator, prepare the mixed berry compote for serving (if using). Combine the berries and lemon juice in a medium saucepan. Heat over medium-high heat for 3-4 minutes.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat slightly and simmer for an additional 2-3 minutes.

Lightly press the berries with a fork or masher to release their juices, avoiding complete mashing. Add the maple syrup and stir well. Adjust with more lemon juice or sweetener as desired. Remove from heat. Serve the berry compote immediately or let it cool for later use.
Once ready to serve, use a knife to loosen the chilled cheesecake from the rim of the springform pan, then remove the rim.
Slice and serve the cheesecake with a spoonful of berry compote, if desired.
notes: A water bath helps prevent cracks and promotes even baking.
Allow the cheesecake to cool slowly in the oven with the door cracked.
Chill the cheesecake overnight for the best texture.
The berry compote is optional but adds brightness and color.
Using slow cooker liners around the springform pan helps prevent leaks.
title: Vanilla Bean Cheesecake with White Chocolate Mousse

author: Kiley Heard
description: These Homemade Beignets with French Hot Chocolate are the ultimate cozy treat. The beignets fry up soft and pillowy on the inside with a perfectly golden, crisp, and slightly chewy exterior, then get buried under a generous snowfall of powdered sugar. Dip them warm into the thick, rich, velvety French hot chocolate that's decadently smooth and topped with marshmallow fluff, homemade whipped cream, and chocolate shavings. It's the dreamiest café-style pairing - cozy, indulgent, and truly unforgettable.
equipment: Large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven
Small bowl or glass measuring cup
Stand mixer with dough hook and whisk attachment
ingredients: 1 cup (240ml) milk of choice, warmed to 110F
2 1/4 tsp (8g; one pack) active dry yeast
6 tbsp (85g) salted butter, at room temperature
2 tsp (8g) vanilla bean paste or extract
Olive or avocado oil, for frying
1 1/2 cups (360ml) whole milk or milk of choice
9oz dark chocolate, chopped (I used 7oz of 72% cacao dark chocolate with 2oz of 60% cacao dark chocolate)
1 tsp (4g) vanilla bean paste or extract
1/2 tsp (2g) vanilla bean paste or extract
instructions: Start by activating the yeast for the beignet dough. Pour the warm milk into a small bowl or glass measuring cup. Add 1 tbsp (12g) of the granulated sugar and sprinkle the yeast overtop. Stir and allow to sit for 5-7 minutes, until frothy and activated.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour and salt. Set aside.

Once activated, pour the yeast mixture into the bowl of a stand mixer (or a large bowl if mixing/kneading the dough by hand). Add the rest of the sugar (1/2 cup minus the 1 tbsp that was used to activate the yeast), eggs, butter, vanilla, and the flour mixture.
With the dough hook attachment (or with a wooden spoon if mixing by hand), mix on low speed for 1 minute, until barely combined. Allow to rest for 5 minutes so the flour can soak up the liquids.

Then, mix on medium speed for 7-8 minutes, until the dough is elastic, smooth, and is pulling away from the sides of the bowl. If the dough seems a little too sticky, you can add 1 tbsp more flour at a time, until it starts to pull away from the sides of the bowl. Don't add too much, you want the dough to be slightly sticky! If you are mixing and kneading by hand, add the rest of the flour mixture to the bowl, mix, and then pour the dough onto a floured surface and knead for 7-8 minutes by hand.
Transfer the dough to a lightly oiled bowl. Cover and allow to rise in a warm environment for 1-1.5 hours, or until doubled in size. I did a full 1.5 hours!

Once the dough is almost finished rising, cut out 16 squares of parchment paper and spread them across 2 baking sheets. These will be used for the beignets to sit on once shaped and make it easier to transfer the beignets to the oil once ready to fry.

Once the dough is doubled, punch it down and turn it onto a lightly floured surface. Roll the dough out to a 10x10” square that is ~1/2” in thickness. Using a pizza cutter or knife, cut the dough into 16 squares (they don't have to be absolutely perfect). Each beignet should measure about 2.5x2.5”.
Place the beignets onto the prepared parchment paper squares on the baking sheets. Loosely cover with a kitchen towel and allow to proof for 30-45 minutes, until puffy.

While the beignets are proofing, prepare the french hot chocolate so it's ready to serve once the beignets are fried. In a medium saucepan over medium heat, add the milk, heavy cream, sugar, cocoa powder, and instant coffee (if using). Stir to combine and heat until heated through. Add the chocolate and whisk until the chocolate is fully melted. Then, remove from the heat and whisk in the vanilla and salt. The hot chocolate should be on the thicker side, if you would like your hot chocolate to be thinner, whisk in more milk until you get your desired consistency. Set aside until ready to serve.
Once the beignets are almost done proofing, fill a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven with oil, filling ~2” in the pan. Heat on medium-high until it reaches 350F-365F.
Line a wire cooling rack with a thick layer of paper towels.

Once the oil is hot enough, carefully transfer the parchment paper squares with the beignets on them slowly into the hot oil, 3-4 at a time. After 10-15 seconds, pull the parchment paper away from the beignets (it should almost immediately release). Fry the beignets for 1-2 minutes on each side (2-4 minutes total), until a golden brown.
Using a slotted spoon, carefully remove the beignets from the oil and transfer to the rack with paper towels. Repeat with the remaining beignets.
Once ready to serve, prepare the whipped cream (if using). Add the heavy whipping cream, powdered sugar and vanilla extract to the of a stand mixer (or mixing bowl).

Use the whisk attachment or a hand-held mixer, and beat the ingredients together, starting on low speed and increasing to high slowly as it begins to firm up. Beat for 60-90 seconds until stiff peaks form. Stopping once halfway through to scrape down the sides of the bowl.
Gently reheat the hot chocolate over the stove (if needed). Serve the beignets warm with a generous dusting of powdered sugar and a cup of the hot chocolate with the whipped cream.
notes: I HIGHLY recommend weighing your ingredients always (it's much more accurate).

If you are using cups, please make sure to scoop and level your flour properly, this is one of the biggest mistakes that occurs when it comes to baking because it's very common to over-measure flour when using cups.
If you are having trouble getting your yeast to activate, make sure to check that your yeast is not expired.
I also highly recommend using a thermometer to ensure that your milk is at the correct temperature.
The hot chocolate can be thinned with additional milk to reach your preferred consistency.
title: Homemade Beignets with French Hot Chocolate

author: Kiley Heard
description: These Pumpkin Cinnamon Roll Pancakes are soft, fluffy, and full of warm pumpkin spice flavor, with caramelized cinnamon sugar swirls that melt into every bite. Cooked to golden perfection and served warm with whipped honey cinnamon butter and a drizzle of maple syrup, they're perfect for a cozy fall breakfast or festive weekend brunch. They taste just like your favorite pumpkin cinnamon rolls-made even easier in pancake form.
ingredients: 2 1/4 cups (540ml) buttermilk, at room temperature [substitution: 2 1/4 cups (540ml) milk of choice whisked with 2 tbsp (30ml) apple cider vinegar or lemon juice]
2 tbsp (28g) salted butter, melted and slightly cooled
2 tbsp of the pumpkin pancake batter
1/2 tsp (2g) vanilla bean paste or extract
instructions: Start by preparing the whipped honey cinnamon butter so it's ready to go once you're ready to serve. In a medium bowl, add the butter, honey, cinnamon, and vanilla. Beat with a whisk or an electric handheld mixer until well combined and fluffy. Set aside and keep at room temperature until ready to serve. You can store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to one month.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, brown sugar, baking powder, baking soda, salt, cinnamon, and pumpkin pie spice (if using). Set aside.
In a separate large bowl, whisk together the buttermilk, pumpkin puree, eggs, melted and slightly cooled butter, and vanilla until smooth.

Add the dry ingredient mixture to the wet, and whisk until just combined (it's okay if the batter is a little lumpy). Set aside and allow the batter rest for 10-15 minutes (do NOT skip - this allows the flour to absorb the liquid, making the pancakes lighter and fluffier).

In a small bowl, mix together the softened (almost melted) butter, sugar, cinnamon, and 2 tbsp of the pumpkin pancake batter until well combined and smooth. Transfer the mixture to a piping bag or a small sandwich baggie with a small portion of the corner/tip snipped off. This is what you're going to use to pipe the cinnamon swirl into the pancakes!
Heat a large nonstick skillet or griddle over medium or medium-low heat (depending on how strong the heat from your stovetop is) and grease with butter.
Use a large cookie scoop or 1/4 cup measuring cup to scoop and pour the pancake batter onto the skillet for each pancake. Let the pancakes cook on the pan for 1 minute.

Then, using the piping bag full of the cinnamon swirl mixture, starting at the middle, gently swirl and create a spiral of the cinnamon swirl mixture into each pancake, pushing slightly into the batter. Try your best to keep the swirl slightly away from the edges so it doesn't melt off the sides (however it's totally okay if it does, this happened to me too)!

Cook for 2-4 minutes, until bubbles appear on the surface. Then use a spatula to gently flip the pancakes over, and cook on the other side for another 1-3 minutes, until golden and cooked through. You can adjust the heat on your stovetop as needed throughout the cooking process if the pancakes seem like they're browning too quickly.
Transfer the cooked pancakes onto a baking sheet and keep warm in the oven at 200F while you make the rest of the pancakes.
Repeat the process with the remaining pancake batter and cinnamon swirl mixture, wiping the pan as needed between each batch.
Once ready, serve the pancakes warm with the whipped honey cinnamon butter and maple syrup, if desired.
notes: This recipe makes a good amount of pancakes, feel free to half the recipe to make a smaller amount!
The batter should rest for 10-15 minutes to produce lighter and fluffier pancakes.
Keep finished pancakes warm in a 200F oven while cooking the remaining batches.
It's okay if some of the cinnamon swirl melts off the edges during cooking.
The whipped honey cinnamon butter can be refrigerated in an airtight container for up to one month.
title: Pumpkin Cinnamon Roll Pancakes (with Whipped Honey Cinnamon Butter)

author: Kiley Heard
description: These Blueberry Lemon Curd Donut Holes are a delicious twist on classic donut holes-made with soft and fluffy blueberry cake donut dough, fried until perfectly golden and crisp, then filled with a bright, sweet-tart lemon curd and coated in a silky vanilla bean buttermilk glaze. A bite-sized burst of flavor, they're best served fresh and warm!
ingredients: 1/2 cup + 2 tbsp (150ml) buttermilk, at room temperature (substitution: milk of choice)
2 tbsp (40g) Greek yogurt or sour cream, at room temperature
2 tbsp (28g) salted butter, melted and slightly cooled
1/2 cup (65g) fresh or frozen blueberries (I used wild blueberries)
3 eggs, at room temperature and beaten
2-4 tbsp (30-60ml) cultured buttermilk (substitution: milk of choice)
instructions: In a large microwave-safe bowl, melt the butter. Then, add the lemon juice, eggs, sugar, and lemon zest. Whisk until combined.

Place the bowl in the microwave and cook for 1 minute. Remove and whisk thoroughly. Microwave again for 30 seconds, remove, and whisk well. Repeat this process 4-6 more times until the mixture is thick enough to coat the back of a spoon. Whisking well after each heating is key!
Once thickened (like custard), allow to cool and then place into the fridge until ready to use.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
In a separate medium bowl, whisk together the buttermilk, egg, greek yogurt, and vanilla until combined. Then, add the melted butter and whisk until fully incorporated.

Add the dry ingredients to the wet, and fold with a spatula or wooden spoon until just combined (the dough will be sticky). Add the blueberries and very gently fold them in until evenly dispersed. Cover the bowl and place in the fridge to chill for at least 30 minutes.
Once chilled and ready to fry, fill a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven with oil, filling ~2” in the pan. Heat on medium-high until it reaches 350F-370F.
Line a wire cooling rack with a thick layer of paper towels.

Once the oil is hot enough, use a small cookie scoop to scoop the dough and drop directly into the hot oil. Add 6-8 donut holes at a time, frying for 2-4 minutes, until golden. I tossed mine using a fork to continually flip them over during the frying process.
Once finished, use a slotted spoon to carefully remove and transfer to the rack with paper towels. Repeat with the remaining dough.

While cooling, make the glaze by whisking together all of the ingredients in a medium bowl, until smooth. If the glaze is too thick, whisk in 1 tbsp more buttermilk at a time. If it's too runny, add more powdered sugar.
Dunk each donut hole into the glaze and toss to coat. Set on a cooling rack until the glaze sets.
Once the donut holes are cooled enough to handle, transfer the lemon curd to a piping bag fitted with a long, filling attachment (you could also just dunk into the lemon curd).
Gently insert the pastry filling tip into the donut holes and squeeze some lemon curd inside.
Serve and enjoy!
notes: Fresh or frozen blueberries both work well.
The dough will be sticky and should chill for at least 30 minutes before frying.
Whisk the lemon curd thoroughly after every microwave interval to prevent scrambling.
Use a thermometer to keep the oil between 350°F and 370°F.
The donut holes are best served fresh and warm.
title: Blueberry Lemon Curd Donut Holes

author: Kiley Heard
description: These Mini Brown Butter Lemon Cakes are soft, light, and fluffy with rich, nutty undertones from the brown butter. Each cake is topped with a cloud of stabilized whipped cream and a swirl of homemade lemon curd that's perfectly tart and sweet. Finished with delicate flowers for a charming, spring-ready touch, they're the perfect balance of flavor and elegance-ideal for brunch, tea parties, or any occasion.
ingredients: 1/2 cup (113g) salted butter, browned and cooled until solidified
1/2 cup (120ml) buttermilk, at room temperature (substitution: milk of choice)
3 eggs, at room temperature and beaten
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350F. Grease a standard 12-cup muffin pan with butter, then dust each muffin well with flour. Set aside.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt. Set aside.
In a separate large bowl, beat together the browned butter and sugar until creamed and lighter in color (~2 minutes).
Add the eggs and vanilla, and beat until well combined.
Add the dry ingredients to the wet, and beat until just barely combined.
Then, add the buttermilk, lemon juice and lemon zest. Whisk until just combined, being careful not to over-mix.
Divide the batter evenly among the muffin wells.
Bake at 350F for 18-20 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the middle comes out clean.
Remove from the oven and allow to cool completely before topping.
In a large microwave-safe bowl, melt the butter. Then, add the lemon juice, eggs, sugar, and lemon zest. Whisk until combined.

Place the bowl in the microwave and cook for 1 minute. Remove and whisk thoroughly. Microwave again for 30 seconds, remove, and whisk well. Repeat this process 4-6 more times until the mixture is thick enough to coat the back of a spoon. Whisking well after each heating interval is key!
Once thickened (like custard), allow to cool and then place into the fridge until ready to use.

Once ready to top the cakes, prepare the whipped cream. Add the cream cheese, sugar, vanilla, and salt to a large bowl, and beat until smooth. Then add the heavy cream and beat until thickened and stiff peaks form.
Once the cakes and lemon curd are cooled, top them with the whipped cream and a dollop of lemon curd. Decorate as desired.
notes: All of the lemon curd will not be used.
Extra lemon curd can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for 1-2 weeks.
Use leftover lemon curd on toast, pancakes, waffles, crepes, or other cakes.
Be careful not to overmix the batter.
Allow the cakes to cool completely before topping.
title: Mini Brown Butter Lemon Cakes

author: Kiley Heard
description: This Sourdough Discard Italian Stromboli is made with the best sourdough discard dough, using a self-rising flour mixture and Greek yogurt for a soft yet crisp crust. It's filled with layers of savory pizza sauce, pesto, roasted red peppers, provolone, salami, pepperoni, and melty mozzarella, then baked until golden and crispy. Served hot with a drizzle of hot honey, this stromboli is the perfect mix of cheesy, spicy, and savory flavors—a must-try for any pizza lover!
ingredients: 1 1/2 cups (213g) '00' flour or all-purpose flour (I highly recommend using a '00' flour)
1/2 cup (145g) sourdough discard (sub: 1/2 cup more Greek yogurt)
instructions: Preheat oven to 400F. Place a pizza stone in the oven, and preheat for at least 30 minutes. This can also be made on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, mix together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, greek yogurt, and sourdough discard until a shaggy dough forms.
Lay the dough out onto a floured surface and knead with your hands until smooth. The dough should be soft and sticky, yet supple.
If the dough seems too wet, add 1 more tbsp of flour at a time, until it comes together more. Shape into a large ball and allow to rest for 10-15 minutes.
Place the dough on a piece of parchment paper that is dusted with flour and roll out into a large rectangle, around 8x12”.

Spread your desired amount of pizza sauce and pesto across the surface of the dough. Arrange the roasted red peppers over the sauce. Then, top with your desired amounts of provolone cheese, salami, pepperoni, and mozzarella cheese.

Brush the edges of the dough with the egg wash. Starting at a short end, tuck in the sides of the dough and carefully roll it up into a log while keeping everything tight and compact. Pinch to seal and place the seam side down.
Brush the top of the stromboli with the egg wash, then sprinkle with sesame seeds, grated parmesan, and Italian seasoning. Take a sharp knife and cut small slits across the top of the dough.

Using a pizza peel or pan, transfer the stromboli onto the heated pizza stone in the oven (I like to keep the parchment paper on the bottom to prevent sticking). Bake at 400F for 20-25 minutes or until the crust is golden brown and crispy.
Remove and let rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
Slice and serve hot with hot honey and extra pizza sauce, if desired.
notes: Please make sure you are using thick GREEK yogurt! The dough will turn out way too sticky if not, it needs the thickness of greek yogurt!
If you need to use dairy free yogurt, I would recommend finding a plant-based Greek-style yogurt, starting with a 1/2 cup of yogurt for the dough and adding more as needed.
A pizza stone gives the crispiest crust, but a parchment-lined baking sheet works well too.
Using '00' flour is highly recommended.
Allow the dough to rest 10-15 minutes before rolling.
title: Sourdough Discard Italian Stromboli

author: Kiley Heard
description: This Sourdough Discard Pizza features a soft and fluffy crust made with sourdough discard, Greek yogurt, and self-rising flour for a light, airy base with a perfectly golden crust. Topped with gooey mozzarella, savory pepperoni, creamy whipped ricotta, fresh basil, hot honey, and a sprinkle of freshly grated parmesan, it's the ultimate pizza that balances flavors and textures in every bite.
equipment: Pizza stone, baking sheet, or cast iron skillet
ingredients: 1 3/4 cups (210g) '00' flour or all-purpose flour (I highly recommend using a '00' flour if possible)
1/2 cup (145g) sourdough discard (sub: 1/2 cup more Greek yogurt)
Pizza oil or olive oil, for brushing
instructions: Preheat oven to 500F. Place a pizza stone in the oven, and preheat for at least 30 minutes. This can also be made on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper or in a cast iron skillet.
In a large bowl, mix together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, greek yogurt, and sourdough discard until a shaggy dough forms.
Lay the dough out onto a floured surface and knead with your hands until smooth. The dough should be soft and sticky, yet supple.

If the dough seems too wet and sticky, add 1 more tbsp of flour at a time, until it comes together more and is moldable. Shape into a large ball and allow to rest for 10-15 minutes. Alternatively, you can place the dough onto a baking sheet that is lined with parchment paper and lightly dusted with flour. Cover with plastic wrap or a kitchen towel and allow to rest for a couple of hours - the dough will rise as a normal pizza dough would and the crust will turn out even fluffier! You can also place into the fridge for up to 2 days.
Place the dough on a piece of parchment paper that is dusted with flour and roll out into a large circle, to your desired thickness.
Use your fingers to fold the edges around the circle to create a crust; gently press down to seal.
Lightly brush the crust and the surface of the pizza with pizza oil or olive oil.
Top the dough with your desired amount of pizza sauce, mozzarella, and pepperoni.

Using a pizza peel or pan, transfer the pizza onto the heated pizza stone in the oven (I like to keep the parchment paper on the bottom to prevent sticking). Bake at 500F for 15-18 minutes or until the cheese has melted and the crust is golden brown.
Add the ricotta, heavy cream, and salt to a blender or food processor. Blend until smooth and creamy, then transfer to a piping bag (optional).
Remove the pizza from the oven and cool for at least 1-2 minutes before transferring to a cutting board. Top with the whipped ricotta, fresh basil, hot honey, and freshly grated parmesan, if desired.
Slice and enjoy!
notes: Please make sure you are using thick GREEK yogurt! The dough will turn out way too sticky if not, it needs the thickness of greek yogurt!
If you need to use dairy free yogurt, I would recommend finding a plant-based greek style yogurt, starting with a 1/2 cup of yogurt for the dough, and adding as needed.
Using '00' flour is highly recommended.
Allowing the dough to rest for a couple of hours will create an even fluffier crust.
The dough can be refrigerated for up to 2 days.
title: Sourdough Discard Pizza

author: Kiley Heard
description: These Salted Honey Butter Sourdough Parker House Rolls are soft, fluffy, and full of flavor! Made with sourdough discard, they're brushed with warm salted honey butter right after baking and sprinkled with flaky sea salt for a perfect sweet-and-salty finish. Ideal for dinner tables, holiday feasts, or any occasion.
ingredients: 1 1/2 tsp active dry or instant yeast
1/2 cup (120ml) warm milk of choice, warmed (~110F)
1 extra egg, beaten, for egg wash
instructions: Start by activating the yeast. In a small bowl, mix together the warm milk, honey, and yeast. Let sit for 5 minutes until the yeast is activated and foamy.

Once activated, add the eggs, melted butter, sourdough discard, salt, and flour. Mix with a dough whisk or wooden spoon until a dough starts to form. Then, use your hands to knead for 5-8 minutes on a lightly floured surface, until the dough is smooth and elastic.
Lightly oil a large bowl, place the dough in, and cover with plastic wrap or a kitchen towel. Allow to rise until doubled in size (45-60 minutes).
Once doubled in size, remove the dough from the bowl and place onto a lightly floured surface.
Divide the dough into 12 equal pieces.

Create a roll out of each dough piece by folding the sides to the center, then roll the ball in a circular motion on a counter surface to create tention, until the top is smooth. Repeat with the rest.
Place the dough balls into a 9x13” baking pan or dish that is greased with butter.
Cover with plastic wrap or a kitchen towel, and allow to proof for 30-45 minutes, until puffed up.
Once proofed, brush with the egg wash.
Bake at 375F for 20-25 minutes, until golden brown.
Make the salted honey butter by mixing together the melted butter and honey in a small bowl.
Remove the rolls from the oven and immediately brush with the salted honey butter.
Sprinkle with flaky sea salt and serve warm.
notes: Mix up the dough, shape the rolls, cover, and refrigerate to slow the rise. They can bake straight from the fridge after several hours.
Another option is to refrigerate the dough for about 12 hours after mixing, then shape and let rise before baking.
The rolls are best served warm.
Instant or active dry yeast both work.
Brushing with the salted honey butter immediately after baking gives the best flavor.
title: Salted Honey Butter Sourdough Parker House Rolls

author: Kiley Heard
description: These Mini Brown Butter Pumpkin Spice Sourdough Cruffins are flaky and buttery, made with homemade sourdough puff pastry (or store-bought for convenience) and filled with a rich brown butter pumpkin spice filling. Coated in cinnamon sugar, they offer the perfect blend of sweet, spiced flavors and crisp texture.
ingredients: 226g (2 sticks) salted butter, cold and grated
1/3 cup salted butter, browned and slightly cooled
1 egg + 1 tbsp milk, for egg wash
1/4 cup granulated sugar + 1 1/2 tsp cinnamon, for rolling
instructions: In a medium bowl, whisk together the sourdough discard and water.

In a separate large bowl, whisk together the flour and salt. Add the grated butter, and mix to coat. Then, make a well in the center of the flour mixture and pour the discard mixture into the center. Stir until a dough starts to form, and then knead with your hands until well combined, and there are no dry bits of flour.
Wrap the dough in a piece of plastic wrap, and flatten it out into a rectangle. Place in the fridge for 30 minutes to chill.

Place the dough onto a heavily floured surface. Roll the dough out into a large rectangle (~18”x8”, it doesn't have to be exact). Perform a book fold by folding the short sides towards the middle so they meet, and then fold the dough in half. Wrap in plastic wrap and chill in the fridge for another 30 minutes.

Then perform 2-3 series of letter folds. Place the dough onto a heavily floured surface, and roll it into a long large rectangle (~24x8”). Fold the sides towards the center, overlapping like you are folding a letter. Wrap in plastic wrap and place in the fridge for 30 minutes between each letter fold.

After the last letter fold, cut two long rectangular strips of parchment paper. Roll the dough into a large rectangle (12”x8”), fold into thirds, and tuck a piece of parchment paper in between the folds to prevent sticking.
Refrigerate the dough for at least 1 hour before using.
Preheat the oven to 375F and grease a standard 12-cup muffin tin with butter; set aside.
In a medium bowl, combine the brown butter, pumpkin puree, brown sugar, flour, pumpkin pie spice, and cinnamon. Mix until well combined, and then place the mixture in the fridge for 10-15 minutes.
Divide the puff pastry in half. Save whichever half you aren't using to use another time, if desired.
Slightly roll out the puff pastry on a sheet of parchment paper. Spread the pumpkin spice filling all over the surface of the puff pastry.
Cut the puff pastry in half lengthwise. Then, cut each strip in half again, and then half again. You should have 8 equal strips of dough.
Roll each strip like a snail, and place into the prepared muffin tin.
Brush the tops of each cruffin with the egg wash.
Bake at 375F for 30-40 minutes, or until they are a deep golden brown.
Allow to cool in the pan for 5 minutes, then remove and immediately roll in a cinnamon sugar mixture to coat.
notes: You can use regular store-bought puff pastry instead. Use 1 sheet in place of the homemade sourdough puff pastry.
To make 16 mini cruffins, use both sheets of the sourdough puff pastry or two sheets of store-bought puff pastry.
The dough requires several chilling periods for proper lamination.
Allow the filling to chill for 10-15 minutes before spreading.
Roll the cruffins in cinnamon sugar while still warm for the best coating.
title: Mini Brown Butter Pumpkin Spice Sourdough Cruffins

author: Kiley Heard
description: Enjoy the perfect blend of crispy and sweet with Sourdough Fried Peaches. These juicy peach slices are dipped in a tangy sourdough batter, fried to golden perfection, and coated in a cinnamon sugar mixture. Served with a rich homemade crème anglaise, this dessert is a delightful twist on classic Southern comfort food.
ingredients: 1 vanilla bean (or 1 tsp vanilla extract), beans removed
3/4 cup whole milk, at room temperature
2 tbsp salted butter, melted and slightly cooled
instructions: In a medium pot over medium heat, add the heavy cream and milk.
Add the vanilla bean seeds, mix, and bring to a simmer.
In a separate medium bowl, add the egg yolks and sugar. Whisk until light in color.

Once the milk mixture is simmering, slowly pour it into the eggs to temper, continuously whisking. Add the mixture back to the pot, and bring back to medium heat. Stir continuously, until slightly thickened. Strain the mixture into a bowl, and place it into the fridge to cool.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, salt, and sugar. Set aside.
In a separate large bowl, whisk together the milk, sourdough discard, and egg until well combined.
Add the dry ingredients to the wet, and whisk. Then, add the melted butter and whisk until just incorporated. Set aside.
Heat at least two inches of oil in a deep saucepan over medium-high heat (350F-375F).
Slice the peaches into eighths, and dust with flour.
Working in batches, drench the peach slices into the batter and slightly shake off any excess. Drop them into the hot oil and fry each side for 1-2 minutes, until golden brown.
Use a slotted spoon to remove, and place on a wire cooling rack that is lined with paper towels.
While still warm, roll the peaches in a cinnamon sugar mixture.
Serve warm with the crème anglaise.
notes: Greek yogurt can be substituted for sourdough discard.
Use a thermometer to maintain oil between 350°F and 375°F.
Straining the crème anglaise creates a smoother texture.
Serve immediately while the peaches are warm and crispy.
Fresh ripe peaches work best.
title: Sourdough Fried Peaches with Crème Anglaise

author: Kiley Heard
description: Enjoy a delicious twist on a classic with Sourdough Tortilla Smash Burger Tacos. These tacos feature juicy smash burger patties that are smashed directly onto homemade sourdough tortillas and cooked until crispy, then topped with melted cheese, crisp lettuce, tangy pickles, and a homemade burger sauce. Perfect for a quick, flavorful meal that combines the best of both tacos and burgers.
ingredients: Cheese of choice (I used grated Gouda)
instructions: Start by preparing the sourdough tortilla dough. In a large bowl, mix together the sourdough discard, olive oil, and water.
Add in the flour and salt, and mix until a dough forms. Knead for 1-2 minutes with your hands, until there are no dry spots and the dough is smooth.
Cover with a damp paper towel, and rest at room temperature for 30 minutes.
While resting, make the burger sauce by combining all of the ingredients in a small bowl, and mixing until well combined. Place into the fridge until ready to use.
Preheat a large cast-iron skillet to medium-high heat.
Lightly flour a surface and turn the dough over. Divide into 10 pieces.
Roll each piece of the dough out into a thin circle (~1/8” thick, it should be almost see through).

Dry cook individually on medium-high heat for ~1 minute on each side. Flip when the bubbles appear and the edges are firm, there should be some nice brown spots! Remove from the pan and place onto a plate or wrap in foil until ready to use.
Portion the meat into 4, 3.5oz balls. Season each with salt and pepper to taste.
Heat a pan over high heat. Once hot, place a ball of seasoned beef onto the pan. Depending on the size of the pan, you can do more than 1 at once, if desired!
Quickly place a sourdough tortilla on top of the beef, and smash it down until very thin, using a large bowl, spatula, or burger press. Cook for ~3 minutes, or until the beef is darkened and crisp.
Use a spatula to flip, reduce the heat to medium, and add the cheese on top of the beef. Cover and allow the cheese to melt, for ~1-2 more minutes.
Remove from the pan and top with lettuce, pickles, and the homemade burger sauce.
notes: The dough makes 10 tortillas, but only 4 are needed for the tacos. Extra tortillas can be saved for later.
Roll the tortillas very thin, almost see-through, for the best results.
A cast-iron skillet helps create a crisp smash burger crust.
Grated Gouda cheese was used in the original recipe, but any melting cheese will work.
The burger sauce can be prepared ahead of time and refrigerated until needed.
title: Sourdough Tortilla Smash Burger Tacos

author: Kiley Heard
description: Indulge in the zesty delight of this Lemon Poppyseed Sourdough Monkey Bread Skillet, a tangy twist on a classic favorite. Crafted with sourdough discard, each fluffy morsel is coated with a lemon sugar and butter mixture, creating a burst of citrus flavor in every bite. Baked to perfection in a skillet, this delightful treat is finished with an almond glaze, adding a touch of sweetness to complement the tartness of the lemon. Perfect for brunch gatherings or a cozy weekend indulgence, this monkey bread skillet is sure to impress.
equipment: 10-inch baking dish or cast iron skillet
ingredients: 3/4 cup Greek yogurt (it has to be Greek yogurt for these measurements)
1/2 cup sourdough discard or unfed starter (sub: 1/4 cup Greek yogurt)
instructions: Preheat the oven to 350F. Grease a 10” baking dish or cast iron skillet with nonstick cooking spray.
In a large bowl, add all of the ingredients for the dough, and mix until well combined. The dough is going to be a little sticky, but just trust the process!

Lay the dough on a generously floured surface. Sprinkle some more flour on top of the dough and use your hands to knead a couple of times, until the dough becomes smooth. If the dough still seems too sticky, add 1 more tbsp of flour at a time, and continue to knead until it comes together more!

Divide the dough into 20 pieces (I just tear a piece off that's about ~1 tbsp, they don't have to be perfectly equal in size). Use your hands and fingers to pinch and form the pieces into balls. Place onto a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and set aside.
Then make the lemon sugar. Toss together the sugar, lemon zest and poppyseeds. Set aside.
Make the lemon butter. In a small bowl, mix together the melted butter and lemon juice.

Roll each ball of dough in the lemon butter mixture, then immediately in the lemon poppyseed sugar mixture to coat, and place into the prepared baking dish. Repeat the process of dipping and rolling with the remaining dough balls, until the pan is almost full (you will probably have to snug some in there).
Bake at 350F for 30-35 minutes, until the top is golden brown.
While baking, whisk together the ingredients for the almond glaze in a medium bowl.
Let the monkey bread cool in the pan for 5-10 minutes, then drizzle the top with the almond glaze.
Serve warm.
notes: This is a sticky dough!
Don't be afraid to flour your hands and sprinkle the outside of the pieces of dough with more flour as you are shaping the dough balls.
Greek yogurt is required for the measurements in this recipe.
Additional flour can be added 1 tablespoon at a time if the dough is too sticky.
Serve warm for the best texture and flavor.
title: Lemon Poppyseed Sourdough Monkey Bread Skillet

author: John Kanell
description: Easy Air Fryer Orange Chicken is about to be your new favorite dinner recipe! It comes together quickly with simple ingredients. Serve it with rice for a satisfying dinner.

groceryIngredients.item: boneless skinless chicken breast or thighs

ingredients: 1 pound boneless skinless chicken breast or thighs, cut into 1-inch cubes
Sesame seeds and sliced green onions, for serving
instructions: In a medium bowl, lightly beat the egg and salt together. Add the cubed chicken and stir to coat the chicken well.

In a shallow bowl, add the cornstarch and stir to break up any large clumps. Work with a few pieces at a time, transfer the chicken pieces to the cornstarch and toss until well coated. (Coat only what will fit in your basket at one time.)
Preheat the air fryer to 400°F.
Place the dredged chicken pieces in a single layer in the air fryer basket. Spray the chicken generously with cooking spray.

Air fry for 6 minutes. Flip the chicken pieces and spray them with cooking spray again. Continue to air fry until golden and crispy, about 6 minutes more. Transfer the cooked chicken to a large bowl, and continue dredging and cooking the remaining pieces.

While the last batch of chicken cooks, combine the orange juice, soy sauce, sugar, vinegar, zest, ginger, oil, pepper, and garlic in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat, and cook until the sugar is dissolved.
Dissolve the cornstarch in the water in a small bowl, and whisk it into the saucepan. Continue cooking, whisking constantly, until thickened and translucent, about 1 minute.
Pour the sauce over the chicken and toss to coat. Serve immediately, topped with sesame seeds and green onions.
notes: Try not to leave any dry spots on the coated chicken. Those areas will turn sticky, not crispy, so coat the chicken well with the spray oil.
Spray the chicken pieces with oil again after flipping them to coat the other side.
Keep the cooked chicken warm while you finish each batch. If cooking in smaller batches, heat an oven or toaster oven to 200°F and place cooked chicken on a wire rack set over a sheet pan.
title: Air Fryer Orange Chicken

author: John Kanell
description: My easy Chicken Parmesan recipe is so easy to make at home. It cooks in under 30 minutes for a dinner of crispy chicken cutlets topped with tomato sauce and melty cheese!
ingredients: Salt and freshly ground black pepper
2 cups low-moisture mozzarella, shredded or thinly sliced (8 ounces, 226g)
Fresh basil and parsley leaves for garnish
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425°F. Butterfly the chicken breasts by cutting through the middle of each lengthwise to create six cutlets. Pound each piece between sheets of plastic wrap using a rolling pin or meat mallet until they are an even thickness.

In a medium bowl, whisk together the eggs with a pinch of salt and pepper. In a separate medium bowl, whisk to combine the flour with 1 teaspoon of salt and ½ teaspoon of pepper. In a third medium bowl, combine the Panko and seasoned breadcrumbs with ½ cup grated Parmesan (28g), chopped parsley and mix together.

Dredge each piece of chicken in the flour mixture and tap off any excess. Dip in the egg and let the excess drip off. Dredge on both sides in the breadcrumb mixture and press down so the breadcrumbs stick and form a nice layer.

In a large skillet, pour the olive oil and heat over medium-high heat until the oil shimmers. Working in batches, add 2 to 3 pieces of chicken to the pan and cook until golden brown on both sides, about 4 minutes per side, adjusting the heat as needed. Transfer to a paper-towel-lined plate to absorb any excess grease.
Transfer to a baking sheet and top each breast with ¼ to ⅓ cup marinara sauce, a generous sprinkle of mozzarella, and about a tablespoon of Parmesan.
Bake in the oven until the chicken is cooked through and the cheese is melted and golden, 15-20 minutes. Remove from the oven and garnish with basil and parsley leaves. Serve over spaghetti.
notes: Pound the chicken cutlets evenly. This ensures they are all roughly the same thickness for even cooking.
Don't have mozzarella? Shredded or sliced provolone cheese works well too!
Chicken breasts are the best cut of chicken for this recipe. Chicken thighs are not recommended.
Have a wet hand and a dry hand when breading the chicken. Use one hand for dry ingredients and the other for the beaten eggs.
If serving with spaghetti, toss the noodles with more marinara sauce or try 2 tablespoons of melted butter and the juice of half a lemon for a brighter, lighter flavor.
title: Chicken Parmesan

author: John Kanell
description: All you need are a few simple ingredients and one pan to make this Chicken Piccata recipe. Filled with bold flavors thanks to the tangy lemon sauce, you'll want to make this again and again.
instructions: Cut chicken breasts in half lengthwise then pound the chicken to flatten out.
Season chicken with salt and pepper then dredge in flour.
Add 2 tablespoons of olive oil and two tbsp butter to a large pan and warm on medium-high heat.
Cook on each side for about 3 minutes or until golden brown.

Add the stock, wine, lemon juice, salt and pepper to the pan then bring to a boil while stirring to deglaze ,reduce to a simmer and add the capers. Allow to reduce by half while stirring occasionally. Remove from heat and stir in 2 tbsp butter. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
Return the chicken to the pan and spoon sauce over the chicken cutlets. You can warm the chicken through on low if needed then sprinkle with parsley and serve.
notes: Slicing the chicken breasts in half and then pounding them ensures that they are thin and will cook through quickly.
If you have a smaller skillet, pan fry the chicken in two batches so the chicken browns instead of steaming.
To get more juice out of a lemon, bring it to room temperature and roll it on the counter under your palm before squeezing.
Don't skip coating the chicken breasts with flour; it gives the chicken a nice crust that the sauce can cling to.
Be careful not to overcook the chicken breasts since they are thin. They are cooked when the internal temperature is 165°F.
personalNotes: This was okay, I think the pan was too hot when cooking. I would try it again.
title: Chicken Piccata

author: John Kanell
description: This Chicken Francese recipe comes together quickly with only a few simple ingredients. An easy chicken dinner for a busy weeknight or for entertaining guests, this chicken recipe is perfect!
instructions: Place the chicken breasts between 2 pieces of plastic wrap. Pound the breasts with a mallet or rolling pin to about ½-inch thickness.
In a shallow dish, combine ¼ cup flour, 1 teaspoon salt, and ½ teaspoon pepper. Place the beaten eggs in another shallow dish.

In a large nonstick skillet, heat the oil over medium-high heat. Dredge two chicken breasts in the flour mixture, then dip in the beaten eggs. Add the chicken to the pan, and cook until golden brown, about 4 minutes. Flip and repeat. Cook until the chicken has an internal temperature of 165F/75C. Remove the cooked chicken to a plate. Keep warm. Repeat the procedure with the remaining two breasts.
Add the lemon slices to the skillet. Cook for 1 minute or until the lemons begin to brown. Transfer the lemon slices to the plate.

Carefully wipe out the skillet with a paper towel. Place the skillet over medium heat. Add the butter and cook until melted. Add 1 tablespoon flour and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly. Gradually whisk in the broth and wine and simmer until the sauce is just thickened, about 4 minutes.

Stir in the lemon juice, remaining ¼ teaspoon salt, and remaining ¼ teaspoon pepper. Return the chicken and lemon slices to the skillet, spooning the sauce over the chicken. Sprinkle with parsley and serve immediately.
notes: Do not skip pounding the chicken breasts. Doing so ensures that they will cook through quickly. Even breasts will stay moist as you don't risk the exterior drying out as the interior cooks through.
To get more juice out of a lemon, bring it to room temperature and roll it on the counter under your palm. Doing so will loosen up the juices so you can more easily squeeze the lemon.
If you had frozen the chicken beforehand, make sure to pat them dry of any moisture. Doing so will help the flour coating stick better to the chicken.
To ensure the chicken is cooked all the way through, use a meat thermometer to check that the chicken has an internal temperature of 165F/75C.
You can use either chicken or vegetable stock for the sauce.
Place cooked chicken in a 200F oven to keep warm while cooking the remaining chicken, lemon slices, and sauce.
title: Chicken Francese

author: John Kanell
description: My 30-minute corn chowder recipe is a must-try for busy weeknights. Thick, creamy, and loaded with sweetness from fresh corn and savoriness from crispy bacon.
ingredients: 3 cups corn kernels, cobs reserved if using fresh (482g)
2 medium Yukon gold potatoes, peeled and diced (369g)
instructions: In a large pot or Dutch oven, add the bacon and cook over medium-high heat until crisp, about 6 minutes, stirring occasionally. Remove the bacon pieces with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels, reserving the dripping in the pot.
Add the butter to the pot and heat until melted. Add the onion, celery, and carrot and cook until tender, about 7 minutes, stirring occasionally. Stir in the garlic and cook for 1 minute.

Add the corn kernels, corn cobs (if using fresh corn), potatoes, broth, milk, salt, thyme, and pepper. (For an extra creamy chowder, replace ½ cup of the milk with heavy cream.) Bring to a simmer, reduce the heat to medium-low, and cook until the potatoes are tender, about 15 minutes, stirring occasionally. Remove from the heat. Remove and discard the corn cobs.
Place 2 cups of the soup mixture in a blender and blend until smooth. Return to pot and stir in the cooked bacon. Serve hot, garnishing each bowl with chopped chives and black pepper.
notes: Watch the heat: Make sure not to bring the soup to a full boil. If boiled, the milk can scorch on the bottom of the pot or curdle.

No-mess method to cut corn off the cob: Use a bundt pan to cut the corn off the cob by standing the cob upright in the hole of the pan. Cut downward with a sharp knife. The corn kernels will fall into the pan and not all over the counter.

If you need to use frozen corn: 3 cups of frozen corn can be substituted if fresh corn is out of season. No need to thaw before adding. Since there will be no corn cobs if you use frozen corn, add a 15-ounce can of creamed corn to the pot when you add the frozen corn. This will give your chowder a more intense corn flavor.

To make vegetarian corn chowder: Water, vegetable stock, or both can be substituted for chicken broth. Leave out the bacon and replace the drippings with 1 tablespoon of butter or olive oil for a vegetarian chowder!
For an extra creamy chowder: Replace 1/2 cup of the milk with half-and-half or heavy cream.

If using a regular blender: Remove the center piece of the lid and place a kitchen towel on top to ensure that there is a way for steam to escape. Otherwise, the top can blow off the top and create a big, steamy mess.
title: Corn Chowder

author: John Kanell
description: A cozy one-pot Potato Soup recipe the whole family will love! Loaded with bacon bits, hearty potatoes, and the perfect amount of creaminess, all you need is a few simple ingredients for this tasty meal!
ingredients: 3 pounds gold potatoes, peeled and chopped into 1-inch pieces (1350g)
instructions: Place the bacon in a large Dutch oven. Cook over medium-high heat, stirring frequently until crisp, about 12 minutes. Using a slotted spoon, remove the bacon pieces and drain on a paper-towel lined plate, leaving the drippings in the pot. Set aside. Reduce the heat to medium.

Remove and reserve half of the bacon drippings. Add the onion and garlic. Cook until the onion turns translucent, about 5 minutes. Stir in the potatoes. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add some reserved bacon drippings if the potatoes start to stick. Sprinkle in the flour and cook, stirring frequently for 2 minutes.
